extracted_codes.context: Algorithm 2 references 'loinc-code == lipid-type' for selecting lipid values from labs-table
Algorithm 3 references 'rxnorm-code∈{ rx-codes}' for selecting medication orders
Algorithm 4 references 'icd9-code∈{ dx-codes}' for selecting diagnoses

clinical_criteria: Lab values must be obtained before earliest exclusion date
Exclusion based on earliest date of hormone therapy treatment (Table 2)
Exclusion based on earliest date of anti-lipid medications (Table 3)
Exclusion based on earliest date of thyroid disorder medications (Table 4)
Exclusion based on earliest date of type 1 or type 2 diabetes mellitus diagnosis (Table 5)
Exclusion based on earliest date of cancer diagnosis (Table 6)
Final result is median of filtered lipid values
algorithm_summary: The Northwestern University basal lipid values selection algorithm extracts valid baseline lipid measurements from EMR data by filtering out values obtained after certain medical conditions or treatments began. The algorithm identifies the earliest dates of exclusionary conditions (hormone therapy, anti-lipid medications, thyroid disorders, diabetes, cancer) and only considers lipid values obtained before these dates. It returns the median of the remaining valid lipid measurements for each patient. The algorithm references specific code tables (Tables 2-6) containing ICD-9, LOINC, and RxNorm codes, though the actual codes are listed in Appendix A which is not included in this document excerpt.
